scores.justification: Machine-checked method with honestly scoped modeling decisions; the near-definitional character of the headline collapse is confronted in Remark 4.6 rather than hidden.
80 kernel-verified results, 0 sorries, 1 inert axiom; both directions of TLP 5.52 proved; audit independently re-ran the build and cross-checked every declaration.
Central claim displayed as a one-sentence quote in the introduction; eight numbered one-sentence contributions with section pointers.

scores.fix: Engage Wehmeier 2004 and Rogers & Wehmeier 2012 (RSL) on Tractarian FOL, identity, and the N-operator; resolver-verify via pub-litsearch before citing.
scores.justification: Closest prior work engaged on its merits (Lokhorst, Weiss, Lampert & Nakano), but the Wehmeier line on the N-operator - including an RSL 2012 paper - is absent from a paper proving N-operator results for RSL.
Pinned toolchain, public repo, CI, step-by-step build appendix; audit reproduced the build from scratch and content-matched the committed PDF.
Both TikZ figures self-contained at caption level, black-only per venue with pattern-based encodings; appendix tables have meaningful headers and booktabs alignment.
Flow intact, calibrated hedging, 0 overfull boxes, 0 broken references per render gate; subject voice tier inactive.
21/21 cites resolve, entries complete with DOIs, claim-support audit clean on on-disk sources; the four off-disk sources are canonical primary texts and the standard Lean system paper.

scores.fix: Compress the ~450-word abstract and the result-by-result conclusion; the result set is narrated three times at full resolution.
scores.justification: Core argument economical and extractable in under 90 seconds, but abstract, contribution list, and conclusion each restate the full result set.

findings.rationale: Wehmeier 2004 and Rogers & Wehmeier 2012 (The Review of Symbolic Logic 5(4)) on Tractarian first-order logic, identity, and the N-operator are not engaged; the 2012 paper is in the target venue and directly concerns the N-operator's expressive reach. Novelty is not misrepresented (that work is paper-based, not machine-checked), so this is a deduction and a related-work lead, not a critical flag.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-run pub-litsearch to resolver-verify the two identifiers and add engagement in Section 5 or Section 4.6.

findings.rationale: Seven-paragraph (~450-word) abstract enumerates every result; ASL/RSL abstracts are customarily terser.
findings.suggested_fix: Fold abstract paragraphs 4-6 into one results paragraph.

findings.rationale: Conclusion re-narrates the abstract and Section 4.3 nearly verbatim - the third full statement of the result set.
findings.suggested_fix: Compress the conclusion to the meaning of the results rather than their restatement.

findings.rationale: scherf2025, an unpublished manuscript with a deleted repository (web.archive snapshot only), is framed as 'the most directly comparable work'.
findings.suggested_fix: Demote the framing or add a published comparison point.

findings.rationale: Prose credits Fogelin with initiating the debate; the critique first appeared in the 1976 first edition, while the entry cites the 1987 second edition (first edition noted parenthetically).
findings.suggested_fix: Cite as Fogelin (1976/1987) at first prose mention.

findings.rationale: 'The three new results' reads as a version-diff leftover; a fresh reader has no baseline for 'new'.
